question: I'm trying to identify a state that would show up in both of these groups: states that fall 4th through 8th when you look for where diabetes-care copays varied the most from one 2024 health insurance plan to another, and states that fall 6th through 10th when you compare the typical electricity prices businesses paid in different ZIP codes served by investor-owned utilities in 2016. Once you've identified that state, how many Medicaid and CHIP beneficiaries there were fully eligible for both Medicaid and Medicare in the final month of 2022? Break any ties alphabetically when forming the intermediate state. Respond with [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/plan-attributes-puf-py2024/files/plan_attributes_PUF.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

state_map = {
    "AL": "Alabama", "AK": "Alaska", "AZ": "Arizona", "AR": "Arkansas", "CA": "California",
    "CO": "Colorado", "CT": "Connecticut", "DE": "Delaware", "FL": "Florida", "GA": "Georgia",
    "HI": "Hawaii", "ID": "Idaho", "IL": "Illinois", "IN": "Indiana", "IA": "Iowa",
    "KS": "Kansas", "KY": "Kentucky", "LA": "Louisiana", "ME": "Maine", "MD": "Maryland",
    "MA": "Massachusetts", "MI": "Michigan", "MN": "Minnesota", "MS": "Mississippi", "MO": "Missouri",
    "MT": "Montana", "NE": "Nebraska", "NV": "Nevada", "NH": "New Hampshire", "NJ": "New Jersey",
    "NM": "New Mexico", "NY": "New York", "NC": "North Carolina", "ND": "North Dakota", "OH": "Ohio",
    "OK": "Oklahoma", "OR": "Oregon", "PA": "Pennsylvania", "RI": "Rhode Island", "SC": "South Carolina",
    "SD": "South Dakota", "TN": "Tennessee", "TX": "Texas", "UT": "Utah", "VT": "Vermont",
    "VA": "Virginia", "WA": "Washington", "WV": "West Virginia", "WI": "Wisconsin", "WY": "Wyoming",
    "DC": "District of Columbia"
}

df["state"] = df["StateCode"].map(state_map)
df["copayment_amount"] = pd.to_numeric(
    df["SBCHavingDiabetesCopayment"].astype(str).str.replace(r"[^0-9.-]", "", regex=True),
    errors="coerce"
)

state_metric = (
    df.dropna(subset=["state", "copayment_amount"])
      .groupby("state", as_index=False)["copayment_amount"]
      .std()
      .rename(columns={"copayment_amount": "stddev_copayment_amount"})
)

ranked = state_metric.sort_values(["stddev_copayment_amount", "state"], ascending=[False, True]).reset_index(drop=True)
answer = ranked.iloc[3:8]["state"].tolist()
1.subquestion: Which states fall 4th through 8th when you look for where diabetes-care copays varied the most from one 2024 health insurance plan to another? Break any ties alphabetically.

2.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/u-s-electric-utility-companies-and-rates-look-up-by-zipcode-2016-87bcc/files/iouzipcodes2016.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

state_map = {
    "AL": "Alabama", "AK": "Alaska", "AZ": "Arizona", "AR": "Arkansas", "CA": "California",
    "CO": "Colorado", "CT": "Connecticut", "DE": "Delaware", "FL": "Florida", "GA": "Georgia",
    "HI": "Hawaii", "ID": "Idaho", "IL": "Illinois", "IN": "Indiana", "IA": "Iowa",
    "KS": "Kansas", "KY": "Kentucky", "LA": "Louisiana", "ME": "Maine", "MD": "Maryland",
    "MA": "Massachusetts", "MI": "Michigan", "MN": "Minnesota", "MS": "Mississippi", "MO": "Missouri",
    "MT": "Montana", "NE": "Nebraska", "NV": "Nevada", "NH": "New Hampshire", "NJ": "New Jersey",
    "NM": "New Mexico", "NY": "New York", "NC": "North Carolina", "ND": "North Dakota", "OH": "Ohio",
    "OK": "Oklahoma", "OR": "Oregon", "PA": "Pennsylvania", "RI": "Rhode Island", "SC": "South Carolina",
    "SD": "South Dakota", "TN": "Tennessee", "TX": "Texas", "UT": "Utah", "VT": "Vermont",
    "VA": "Virginia", "WA": "Washington", "WV": "West Virginia", "WI": "Wisconsin", "WY": "Wyoming",
    "DC": "District of Columbia"
}

df["state_full"] = df["state"].map(state_map)
df["ind_rate_num"] = pd.to_numeric(df["ind_rate"], errors="coerce")

state_metric = (
    df.dropna(subset=["state_full", "ind_rate_num"])
      .groupby("state_full", as_index=False)["ind_rate_num"]
      .median()
      .rename(columns={"ind_rate_num": "median_ind_rate"})
)

ranked = state_metric.sort_values(["median_ind_rate", "state_full"], ascending=[False, True]).reset_index(drop=True)
answer = ranked.iloc[5:10]["state_full"].tolist()
2.subquestion: Which states fall 6th through 10th when you compare the typical electricity prices businesses paid in different ZIP codes served by investor-owned utilities in 2016? Break any ties alphabetically.

3.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/dual-status-information-for-medicaid-and-chip-beneficiaries-by-year-405b3/files/DualStatus-anul.txt"
state_name = "Kansas"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

filtered = df[
    (df["State"] == state_name)
    & (pd.to_numeric(df["Year"], errors="coerce") == 2022)
    & (df["DualStatus"] == "Full dual eligibility")
].copy()
filtered["CountLastMonthEnrollment"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["CountLastMonthEnrollment"].astype(str).str.replace(",", "", regex=False), errors="coerce")

answer = int(filtered.iloc[0]["CountLastMonthEnrollment"])
3.subquestion: In 2022, how many Medicaid and CHIP beneficiaries in <hop 1 answer> were fully eligible for both Medicaid and Medicare in the final month of the year?

reasoning: Node 1: Map `StateCode` to full state names, extract numeric copayment values from `SBCHavingDiabetesCopayment`, group by state to compute the sample standard deviation of copayment amount, sort by standard deviation descending and state ascending to break ties alphabetically, and take ranks 4 through 8.

Node 2: Map `state` abbreviations to full state names, convert `ind_rate` to numeric, group by state to compute the median industrial electricity rate, sort by median descending and state ascending to break ties alphabetically, and take ranks 6 through 10.
Hop result: semantic intersection of <node 1 answer> and <node 2 answer> = Kansas.

Node 3: Filter rows where `State` = <hop 1 answer>, `Year` = `2022`, and `DualStatus` = `Full dual eligibility`, convert `CountLastMonthEnrollment` to numeric after removing commas, and report the final-month enrollment count.
Hop result: Kansas had 56155 beneficiaries with full dual eligibility in the final month of 2022.
